summary: Iteration 6 — CPA draft upload + cancel + auto IN_PREP↔IN_REVIEW transitions and client ReviewDecisionCard polish. Backend 13/13 PASS in test_draft_review_flow.py covering: (1) IN_PREP upload-draft auto-moves to IN_REVIEW, sets t2_draft_doc_id, saves review_instructions, returns moved_to_review:true; (2) IN_REVIEW re-upload clears prior review_decision and keeps status (moved_to_review:false); (3) DELETE /engagements/{eid}/draft removes doc, unsets t2_draft_doc_id+review_decision, reverts to IN_PREP (moved_back_to_prep:true); (4) DELETE 404 when no draft; (5) DELETE 403 for CLIENT and WS_PARTNER, 200 for ADMIN; (6) Regression: POST /move-to-review still callable; POST /review-decision approved + issue (with empty note → 400) all green. Frontend (CPA side) verified live: /cpa/files → Open Rachel Thompson → UploadDraftCard renders with submit labelled exactly 'Send and Move to Review' (NOT 'Upload draft'); zero 'Save instructions' buttons in DOM; uploading a PDF + instructions auto-advanced engagement to IN_REVIEW (kanban row flipped to 'In review' 15/15 docs); existing-draft strip shows Download + new ✕ cancel-draft button; clicking ✕ → confirm dialog accepted → existing-draft strip removed and engagement reverted (verified via re-render). Client-side ReviewDecisionCard CSS audit done via code review (ClientPortal.js 256-360): no emojis, Lucide ThumbsUp/Flag size=16 strokeWidth=1.75, optionBase border = var(--border-default) with hover-only green/red accent via onMouseEnter, issue textarea uses standard .textarea class (neutral white), submit button uses btn-primary (blue). E2E loop (CPA upload → client issue → CPA re-upload → fresh prompt) is fully covered by backend test test_inreview_reupload_keeps_status_and_clears_decision.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py upload_draft_pdf correctly $unset's review_decision on every upload (line 1807) — guarantees client always sees a fresh prompt for a new draft. Verified by E2E backend test.

delete_draft_pdf at lines 1828-1856 properly: (a) deletes local file bytes if storage=local, (b) removes the document doc, (c) $unset's t2_draft_doc_id+review_decision, (d) reverts IN_REVIEW→IN_PREP only (no-op for other statuses), (e) logs status change. Clean implementation.
Status guard at line 1748 ('IN_PREP', 'IN_REVIEW') correctly blocks draft uploads in REFERRED/INTAKE/DELIVERY/FILED — good defensive check.
UploadDraftCard.submit() (CpaEngagement.js line 35) sends both file and instructions in a single onUpload call — eliminates the separate 'Save instructions' button as designed.
ReviewDecisionCard uses inline onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave for hover styling — works but a CSS-class-based hover would be more maintainable; cosmetic, not blocking.
server.py is now 2310 lines — split into routers strongly recommended (carry-over from iter 4/5).
DELETE /engagements/{eid}/draft has no idempotency on the underlying status revert (correct — it only reverts when in IN_REVIEW). 404 on missing draft is the correct surface for the cancel-X UI.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_draft_review_flow.py (NEW — 13 pytest tests covering upload-draft auto-transition, delete-draft RBAC + status revert, regression on review-decision and move-to-review)

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 passed in 24.6s — auth sanity 3, upload-draft auto-move 2, delete-draft 5, regression 3)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on CPA-side verified live: upload-draft-card visible, submit label 'Send and Move to Review', zero Save-instructions buttons, existing-draft + Download + cancel-X all render after upload, cancel-X removes draft and engagement reverts (kanban shows 'In review' after upload then back). Client ReviewDecisionCard verified by code review (no emoji, Lucide icons, neutral default + hover accent, neutral textarea, blue primary submit) — backend e2e loop test confirms review_decision clears on CPA re-upload.

seed_data_creation: No new users created. Tests modify Thompson's engagement (cc22901b-b6a2-4997-b84a-a7bfc2b307f0) — the autouse cleanup fixture deletes any leftover draft so the engagement is left at IN_PREP w/o draft. Note: live UI test uploaded a final draft so Thompson is currently IN_REVIEW with a draft — main agent or next testing agent can DELETE /api/engagements/{eid}/draft to revert if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: IMPORTANT: Thompson (thompson@example.com) is assigned to terryann@cloudtax.ca, NOT pallavi@cloudtax.ca — use Terry-Ann for any CPA-side tests on Thompson's engagement. After this run Thompson's engagement is in IN_REVIEW with a draft uploaded by the live UI test (file ui_test_draft3.pdf or similar). Run cpa_session.delete('/api/engagements/cc22901b-b6a2-4997-b84a-a7bfc2b307f0/draft') to reset to IN_PREP if needed for next iteration.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Both backend endpoints (POST /upload-draft auto-move + clear, DELETE /draft) and frontend components (UploadDraftCard 'Send and Move to Review' button + cancel-X, ReviewDecisionCard neutral baseline) work as specified.

mocked_apis.aws_s3: S3 PutObject fails (user IAM lacks s3:PutObject) — backend correctly falls back to local storage at /app/backend/uploads/{eid}/draft/. This is documented expected behavior in the request notes.
